comprehension_cronbach.cronbach_notes: Cronbach requires items with scores on ALL dimensions; null dimensions exclude that row. The concept grader correctly uses null for unaddressed dimensions, so Cronbach is usually insufficient-data — the Spearman matrix below is the primary reliability signal.

spearman.notes: Pairwise Spearman with pairwise-deletion of missing data. Per-dim mean off-diagonal correlation is the per-dim reliability proxy: values near 1.0 imply the dimension is redundant with the others; values near 0 imply orthogonal. A healthy grader has 0.3-0.7 mean off-diagonal correlation — dims should correlate (measuring related concepts) but not collapse (each adds signal).
